features.title: Source-backed release context
features.description: Pull requests, linked issues, rollout notes, and QA context stay tied to the draft instead of being rebuilt across chat, docs, and handoff threads.

features.title: Visible evidence links
features.description: Teams can inspect where a customer-facing claim came from before approving it for publication.

features.title: Shipped scope snapshots
features.description: Scope stays grounded in what actually shipped so public wording does not drift beyond the release record.

features.title: Claim checks
features.description: Flag unsupported certainty, missing evidence, and phrasing that belongs in internal rollout notes rather than customer communication.

features.title: Reviewable wording changes
features.description: Teams can compare edits and rationale in one place instead of reconstructing wording decisions later.

features.title: Human sign-off
features.description: Engineering, support, and product marketing responsibility stays explicit before anything leaves review.

features.title: Public release notes
features.description: Explain what changed, why it matters, and what customers can do now without overstating the evidence.

features.title: Support brief
features.description: Carry rollout caveats, known edges, and likely customer questions forward for support and success teams.

features.title: Stakeholder summary
features.description: Keep internal stakeholders aligned on shipped scope, approved wording, and evidence references.
